---
title: "Evolutionary Dynamics Simulations Examining the Effect of Genotype-specific Fitness on Population-level Allele Frequencies"
author: Eric Gong
output:
  pdf_document:
    latex_engine: xelatex
documentclass: article
header-includes:
  - \usepackage{microtype}
  - \pretolerance=100
  - \tolerance=2000
  - \emergencystretch=1em
  - \hyphenpenalty=1500
  - \AtBeginDocument{\sloppy}
  - \usepackage{placeins}
  - \usepackage{etoolbox}
  - \pretocmd{\section}{\FloatBarrier}{}{}
  - \pretocmd{\subsection}{\FloatBarrier}{}{}
  - \pretocmd{\subsubsection}{\FloatBarrier}{}{}
---
# Abstract
after documentclass:
classoption:
  - twocolumn

# 1 Background and Introduction

# 2 Simulation Methodology

# 3 Results and Discussions

# 4 Conclusion

# 5 Sources

# 6 Notes

Model weights are normally distributed, centered at 0 in 92.5\% of tests: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2305.14314

Neuzip: about 3 bits of info in 8 exponential bits, but 8 out of 8 bits used for mantissa: https://arxiv.org/html/2410.20650v1

about 2.6 bits of info in 8 exponential bits https://research.cloudflare.com/papers/unweight-2026.pdf
 
Strong outliers exist past 6.7B parameters, about 20x magnitude, about 0.1\% https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2022/file/c3ba4962c05c49636d4c6206a97e9c8a-Paper-Conference.pdf

ZipLLM: deltas or xors are small https://arxiv.org/html/2505.06252v3

LORA changes 0.01\% of weights: https://arxiv.org/abs/2106.09685
